content.text: This policy applies only to the product "Blotz" and its related services provided by Blotz (hereinafter referred to as "we").

The security of your personal information is very important to us. We will collect and use your personal information in accordance with applicable laws and regulations to help us provide you with better products and services. We are committed to keeping your personal information and other data strictly confidential.
Please read this Privacy Policy (hereinafter referred to as "this Policy") carefully before using our products and services. By using Blotz products and related services, you agree to this Policy.
Contents
I. How We Collect and Use Your Personal Information
II. How We Use Cookies and Other Technologies
III. How We Share, Transfer, and Publicly Disclose Your Personal Information
IV. How We Store Your Personal Information
V. How We Protect Your Personal Information
VI. Your Rights to Manage Personal Information
VII. How We Handle Personal Information of Minors
VIII. How This Policy Is Updated
IX. How to Contact Us
I. How We Collect and Use Your Personal Information

We follow the principles of legitimacy, legality, and necessity, and collect and use personal information you voluntarily provide while using our products or services for the following functions and services.
(I) Account Registration
When you register and log in to your Blotz account, you may use the following methods:

Email registration: To complete account registration and allow you to log in to Blotz via email, we will collect the email address you provide. We will also send a verification email to verify the authenticity of the address.

Third-party account registration: To complete account registration and allow you to quickly log in to Blotz using a third-party account, when you choose to register with a third-party account (such as Apple or Google), we will obtain certain basic information from your third-party platform profile (such as nickname, avatar, and email address) pursuant to the authorization agreement with that platform, to create your Blotz account.

Please note: Blotz does not offer a local mode that does not require account registration. You must register an account to use all features of Blotz, and your task data will be synced across your devices.
(II) App Features
1. Task Data Sync
Once you register an account and create tasks and other data, to enable complete data synchronization across your devices, you need to allow us to collect:
Your task title, task content, task priority, task due date (or time period), task reminder time, and task recurrence rules
Your quick note content
Device information for editing operations (including: device model, operating system version, and unique device identifier)
The above information is used solely to sync your task data across devices. We will not read your task content for other purposes without your authorization.
2. Task Reminders

When you set a reminder for a task in Blotz, we will launch the app to send the reminder when the time arrives. For this purpose, we need to collect: task title, task content, task due date (or time period), and task reminder time.
3. Location-Based Reminders

After you set a location-based reminder for a task and grant Blotz access to "Location" permissions, to ensure Blotz can remind you when you arrive at or leave a location, we may collect: the coordinates of your saved locations.
4. Search Feature

When you use the search feature in Blotz, to help you review recent search history, we may collect: the keywords you search for. Your search keywords are only stored locally on the client and will not be synced to the cloud.
5. AI Features
Some features in Blotz are powered by artificial intelligence. We have integrated Microsoft's Azure OpenAI service.

When you actively use AI features, we only process content that you actively input or select, such as task titles, task details, quick note content, tag information, and date information. This data is transmitted to Azure OpenAI to generate content or suggestions relevant to your instructions.

Regarding data retention: According to Microsoft Azure OpenAI's default data processing policy, your inputs (prompts) and outputs (completions) will not be used by Microsoft to train models, nor will they be persistently stored after the API request is complete. For the specific retention policy, please refer to Microsoft's official documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/legal/cognitive-services/openai/data-privacy

Your input may be referenced, rewritten, or combined in AI outputs to better complete the task you specified. All processing is limited to what is necessary to deliver the feature. We will not use it for any purpose unrelated to AI features, nor will we use this data to train models.

You may choose whether or not to use any AI features. If you choose not to, we will not trigger any related information collection, processing, or transmission to third parties, and it will not affect your use of other basic services.
6. Profile

You may voluntarily provide and allow us to collect the following information by completing your profile: avatar, nickname. This information is not required for core business functions or system operation. We will not require you to provide it, and declining will not adversely affect your use of Blotz.
(III) In-App Permission Requests

We need to request certain system permissions that involve personal privacy to ensure some in-app features can function properly. We will request your authorization when you use these features, and only after obtaining your explicit authorization will we access your relevant information. If you do not need to use these features, you may decline to grant authorization.
1. Permissions Requested on Android
Calendar access: We will request this permission when you enable the "Calendar Sync" feature. Once granted, you can sync system calendar events to Blotz.

Approximate and precise location: We will request this permission when you use the "Location Reminder" feature. Once granted, you can set location reminders for tasks, and Blotz will notify you when you arrive at or leave a selected location.
Write to external storage: We will request this permission when you save task attachments to your phone.
Read from external storage: We will request this permission when you upload attachments to tasks.
Microphone: We will request this permission when you add audio attachments to tasks or use voice input.
Fingerprint: We will request this permission when you set up a fingerprint lock. Once granted, you can use your fingerprint to unlock the app.
Clipboard: When you enable the "Recognize Clipboard Content" feature, if the clipboard contains time information, the app will automatically ask if you want to add a task when you open the app.
2. Permissions Requested on iOS
Calendar access: We will request this permission when you enable the "Calendar Sync" feature.
Approximate and precise location: We will request this permission when you use the "Location Reminder" feature.
Write to external storage: We will request this permission when you save task attachments to your phone.
Read from external storage: We will request this permission when you upload attachments to tasks.
Microphone: We will request this permission when you add audio attachments to tasks or use voice input.
Face ID: We will request this permission when you set an unlock password and enable "Face ID" unlock. Once granted, you can use Face ID to unlock the app.
Clipboard: When you enable the "Recognize Clipboard Content" feature, if the clipboard contains time information, the app will automatically ask if you want to add a task when you open the app.
Siri: We will request this permission when you enable the "Add Tasks via Siri" feature. Once granted, you can speak to Siri directly to add tasks.
(IV) Third-Party SDKs and Services
Blotz uses the following third-party libraries and services to support core functionality:
1. Backend Services (Microsoft / NuGet)

Our backend services are built on NuGet component libraries provided by Microsoft, primarily used for server-side logic, data storage, and API communication. Data processing complies with Microsoft's privacy policy: https://privacy.microsoft.com
2. Frontend Framework (React Native / Expo)

Our mobile app frontend is built on React Native and Expo frameworks. These frameworks do not independently collect user personal information and are used solely to implement app interfaces and interaction features.
3. AI Service (Microsoft Azure OpenAI)

As described in the AI Features section above, we have integrated Microsoft Azure OpenAI for AI functionality. Azure OpenAI servers are located in US East. Please refer to Microsoft's privacy policy for details.
(V) User Research and Online Engagement

We may conduct user research from time to time, primarily through surveys. To better serve different user groups, we may collect the following personal information from respondents: age, gender, industry, occupation, device information, and personal interests.

We may also hold interactive activities on social media from time to time. To facilitate user participation and prize distribution, we may collect the following personal information from participants: name, contact phone number, and contact address.

The above information is used solely for specific activities. We will not use this personal information for other purposes. If you do not wish to provide such information, you may decline to participate in our research or interactive activities.
II. How We Use Cookies and Other Technologies

A Cookie is a small text file placed on your device by a website server when you browse a web page. When you visit the website again, the website can recognize your browser through the Cookie. Cookies may store user preferences and other information.

Blotz uses Cookies as a convenience feature, allowing users to move from one Blotz service to another without having to re-authenticate. We use Cookies to: store user preference settings, improve user experience, and analyze usage patterns to help us improve service quality.
You can refuse Cookies by modifying your browser or device settings. If you choose to refuse Cookies, you may not be able to log in or use Blotz services or features that rely on Cookies.
III. How We Share, Transfer, and Publicly Disclose Your Personal Information
(I) Sharing
Without your consent, we will not share your personal information with any other companies, organizations, or individuals, except in the following circumstances:

With your explicit consent or authorization: After obtaining your explicit authorization, we may share your personal information with other parties (including AI service providers such as Microsoft Azure OpenAI).

Sharing under legal circumstances: As required by laws and regulations, for resolving litigation disputes, or pursuant to mandatory requirements from government authorities or judicial rulings, we may be required to share your personal information externally.
To protect the interests, property, or safety of Blotz, its users, or the general public from harm, it may be necessary to provide your personal information to third parties.
(II) Transfer
Without your consent, we will not transfer your personal information to any company, organization, or individual, except in the following circumstances:
With your explicit consent or authorization.

In the event of a merger, acquisition, or bankruptcy liquidation, personal information transfer may be involved. In such cases, we will require the new company or organization holding your personal information to continue to be bound by this Policy.
(III) Public Disclosure
Without your consent, we will not publicly disclose your personal information, except in the following circumstances:
With your explicit consent or authorization.

Under legal requirements, legal processes, litigation, or mandatory requirements from government authorities, we may disclose your personal information to competent authorities, taking security measures that meet legal and industry standards.
IV. How We Store Your Personal Information
(I) Retention Period

During your use of this platform, we will continuously store your personal information, with the retention period not exceeding what is necessary to provide services to you. After you stop using this platform or revoke the relevant authorization, except where laws and regulations require retention of specific information for a longer period, we will delete or anonymize your information.
(II) Storage Location

Our primary servers are located in Australia. Where certain features involve cross-border data transfer (such as the Microsoft Azure OpenAI service used for AI features, whose servers are located in US East), we will strictly comply with applicable laws and regulations to ensure the security of your personal information.
V. How We Protect Your Personal Information

The security of your personal information is extremely important to us. We will strictly comply with applicable laws and regulations and take reasonable and feasible measures recognized by the industry to protect your personal information from unauthorized access, disclosure, use, modification, damage, or loss.

We have implemented industry-standard security measures to protect your personal information, such as using SSL/TLS encryption during data transmission, using encryption technology to ensure data confidentiality, and deploying access control mechanisms to ensure that only authorized personnel can access personal information.

In the event of a personal information security incident, we will promptly notify you of the basic situation, potential impact, measures taken or to be taken, and remedies available to you, in accordance with applicable laws and regulations. We will notify you via email, push notifications, and other means.
VI. Your Rights to Manage Personal Information
(I) Access and Correct Your Personal Information
You have the right to access and correct your personal information. You can do so through the following methods:
Profile: Go to "Settings" → "Account" to access or modify your avatar, nickname, email account, and login password.
Search history: Tap Search to access or clear your recent search keywords.
(II) Delete Your Personal Information
You may request deletion of personal information in the following circumstances:
If our handling of personal information violates laws or regulations;
If we collect or use your personal information without your consent;
If you no longer use our products or services, or if you cancel your account;
If we no longer provide products or services to you.

You can delete your account and all personal data by going to "Settings" → "Account" → "Delete Account". After your account is deleted, we will stop providing services to you and delete your personal information as requested (except where otherwise required by laws and regulations).
(III) Change the Scope of Your Authorization

For additionally collected personal information, you may grant or withdraw your consent at any time. After you withdraw consent, we will no longer process the corresponding personal information, but this will not affect the processing of personal information conducted based on your prior authorization.
(IV) Revoke System Permissions
You can revoke system permissions previously granted to us through your device's system settings:
Android: System Settings → App Management → Blotz → Permissions
iOS: System Settings → Privacy & Security → Select the permission type → Disable Blotz access; or: System Settings → Apps → Blotz → Disable the relevant permission.

After you revoke a permission, we will no longer process relevant personal information based on that permission. The corresponding feature may no longer function properly, but other basic features will not be affected.
(V) Responding to Your Requests
We will respond to your reasonable requests within 15 days. If you need assistance, please contact us through the contact information at the end of this Policy.
VII. How We Handle Personal Information of Minors

Our products and services are primarily intended for adults. If you are under 18 years of age, please read this Policy with the accompaniment of a guardian and only use our services after obtaining the guardian's consent.
If we discover that we have collected personal information from a minor without prior verifiable consent from a guardian, we will endeavor to delete the relevant data as soon as possible.
VIII. How This Policy Is Updated

This Privacy Policy may be updated from time to time. You should check it periodically to understand our approach and procedures for protecting the personal information we collect. Your continued use of Blotz services constitutes your agreement to this Privacy Policy and any updated versions.
For significant changes, we may also provide more prominent notice describing the specific changes to this Policy. Significant changes include but are not limited to:
Significant changes to our service model, such as the purpose, type, or manner of processing personal information;
Significant changes in ownership structure or organizational structure;
Changes to the main parties with whom personal information is shared, transferred, or publicly disclosed;
Significant changes to your rights regarding personal information processing and how to exercise them;
Changes in the department responsible for personal information security, contact information, or complaint channels.
IX. How to Contact Us
If you have any questions, comments, or suggestions about this Privacy Policy, please contact us through the following:
Send an email to: blotztask.app@gmail.com
We will respond within 15 days of receiving your feedback.
